No Poverty.reason: The course's focus on policy evaluation for government and non-profits directly supports creating sound policy frameworks for poverty reduction (Target 1.b).
evidence: 政府與非營利組織的績效評估
政策分析與機構管理的架構

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or inferable evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course explicitly uses 'financial statement analysis of non-profit hospital' as an application case, directly linking to health system efficiency (Target 3.8).
evidence: 應用案例：非營利醫院財務報表分析
Application: Financial statement analysis of non-profit hospital

Quality Education.reason: No explicit or strong inferable evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to education quality, access, or learning outcomes. General performance evaluation is too broad.

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or inferable evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to gender equality, women's empowerment, or discrimination.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or inferable evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to water, sanitation, hygiene, or water resource management.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or inferable evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to energy, renewables, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course explicitly teaches cost-benefit analysis, efficiency, and productivity measurement, which are direct tools for achieving higher economic productivity (Target 8.2).
evidence: 成本效益分析 (CBA)
衡量效率與生產力
以經濟效率的角度比較各項方案的良窳

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course's focus on cost-benefit analysis, especially incorporating externalities, provides a strong framework for planning sustainable and resilient infrastructure and industries (Targets 9.1, 9.4).
evidence: 成本效益分析 (CBA)
計畫評估的原則
將外部性、市場結構等機經濟概念納入成本效益分析

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course provides policy analysis and performance evaluation tools for government, which can be applied to design and assess fiscal and social policies aimed at reducing inequalities (Target 10.4).
evidence: 政策分析與管理
政府與非營利組織的績效評估

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: Cost-benefit analysis and the incorporation of externalities, taught in the course, are foundational tools for sustainable urban planning and managing environmental impacts in cities (Targets 11.3, 11.6).
evidence: 成本效益分析 (CBA)
將外部性...納入成本效益分析

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course explicitly teaches efficiency/productivity measurement, analysis of non-market goods, and incorporating externalities, which are crucial for sustainable resource management and consumption (Target 12.2).
evidence: 衡量效率與生產力
納入非市場化商品的分析
將外部性...納入成本效益分析

Climate Action.reason: The course's focus on incorporating externalities and risk into policy analysis provides essential tools for integrating climate change measures into national planning and strengthening resilience (Targets 13.1, 13.2).
evidence: 將外部性...納入成本效益分析

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or inferable evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or sustainable fisheries.

Life on Land.reason: The course's methods for valuing non-market goods and analyzing externalities provide tools to integrate ecosystem and biodiversity values into national planning (Target 15.9).
evidence: 納入非市場化商品的分析

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course's core focus on performance evaluation and policy analysis for government and non-profit organizations directly contributes to developing effective, accountable, and transparent institutions (Target 16.6).
